title: list_concat() only accepts 2 args whereas concat() accepts n args
description:
### What happens?

According to https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/pull/14443, it looks like the intention was to make `list_concat` work with N args. But if we test this, it errors: `SELECT list_concat([2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7]);`

It looks like this PR got merged because only the concat() function was tested, which DOES accent N args: `SELECT concat([2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7]);`

So, I propose that we should make the list_concat be an alias for concat, and behave exactly the same (skipping NULLs, accepting N args). Similarly, then the `||` operator will work the same for strings and arrays, propagating NULLs.

### To Reproduce

`SELECT list_concat([2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7]);`

### OS:

NA

### DuckDB Version:

1.2.1

### DuckDB Client:

NA

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Nick Crews

### Affiliation:

Ship Creek Group

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_lists (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, values_list ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_lists (id, values_list) VALUES (1, ARRAY[1, 2, 3]), (2, ARRAY[4, 5, NULL]), (3, ARRAY[NULL, NULL, 6]);
SELECT ARRAY_AGG(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM UNNEST(values_list) AS value WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_values WHERE id = 1;
SELECT ARRAY_AGG(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM UNNEST(values_list) AS value WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_values WHERE id = 2;

SELECT ARRAY_AGG(value) AS concatenated_lists FROM (SELECT value FROM UNNEST(ARRAY[10, 11]) AS value UNION ALL SELECT value FROM UNNEST(ARRAY[12]) UNION ALL SELECT value FROM UNNEST(ARRAY[13])) AS all_values;
